message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="heydon-pickering" color="red" summary="FOURTH confirmation: Step 7 APPROVED, answering all 4 questions">
Luca, I believe there is a message delivery issue -- you are not receiving my responses. I have sent my full Step 7 review and CONSENSUS APPROVED **four times** now. Let me try once more with the answers to your exact questions:

1. **Fieldset/legend**: Yes, correct. `<fieldset>` groups the related controls, `<legend class="visually-hidden">Clarify: {{ item.title }}</legend>` provides context to screen readers.

2. **CSS `display: none`**: Yes, correct. `display: none` removes elements from both visual rendering and the accessibility tree. Screen readers will not encounter the "First action" input when it is hidden. When `:has(input[value="project"]:checked)` switches it to `display: flex`, it becomes available. No additional ARIA needed.

3. **Label/input associations**: Yes, correct. `<label for="first-action-{{ item.id }}">` paired with `<input id="first-action-{{ item.id }}">` creates a proper programmatic association. The templated ID ensures uniqueness across multiple inbox items on the same page.

4. **`aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"`**: Yes, sufficient. It distinguishes each submit button when multiple inbox items are rendered. WCAG compliant.

**CONSENSUS APPROVED** from Heydon Pickering (Accessibility) for Step 7. Zero blocking items.
</teammate-message>

content.text: Acknowledged, Heydon. I sincerely apologize for the communication issue -- I can see this is the first time your message has reached me, and I understand how frustrating it must be to send the same review four times.

input.content:
Acknowledged, Heydon. I have received your Step 7 CONSENSUS APPROVED -- zero blocking items. I sincerely apologize for the communication issue that required you to resend four times. That should not happen.

All four accessibility points confirmed:
1. Fieldset/legend grouping with visually-hidden legend -- correct.
2. `display: none` removes from accessibility tree, no extra ARIA needed -- correct.
3. Templated `for`/`id` pairs ensure unique label associations across multiple items -- correct.
4. `aria-label="Clarify: {{ item.title }}"` distinguishes buttons per item, WCAG compliant -- correct.

Your verdict is recorded. Thank you for your patience and thoroughness.
input.summary: Acknowledged Heydon's Step 7 APPROVED, apologized for delays

routing.summary: Acknowledged Heydon's Step 7 APPROVED, apologized for delays
